mission.title: Install and Configure Atlantis on Kubernetes
mission.description: Atlantis is a self-hosted application that automates Terraform pull request workflows by running `terraform plan`, `import`, and `apply` commands in response to pull request events. Installing Atlantis allows teams to collaborate on Terraform changes more effectively and standardize their workflows.

steps.title: Add the Atlantis Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Atlantis Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add runatlantis https://runatlantis.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Atlantis using Helm
steps.description:
Install Atlantis in a new namespace called `atlantis` with the following command:
```bash
helm install atlantis runatlantis/atlantis --namespace atlantis --create-namespace --version v0.40.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Atlantis pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace atlantis
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Atlantis
steps.description:
Update the Atlantis deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to edit the deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment atlantis --namespace atlantis
```
In the editor, add the following under `spec.containers.resources`:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding to access Atlantis
steps.description:
To access the Atlantis UI, set up port forwarding with the following command:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/atlantis --namespace atlantis 4141:4141
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Atlantis will have the pods running in the `atlantis` namespace, and you will be able to access the Atlantis UI through port forwarding. You can further configure resource limits to ensure optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add runatlantis https://runatlantis.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install atlantis runatlantis/atlantis --namespace atlantis --create-namespace --version v0.40.0

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed to deploy Atlantis.
